errorState.title: Workbench Unavailable
errorState.description: We couldn't load the project layout data for this rooftop.

mobileGuard.title: Open this on a larger screen
mobileGuard.subtitle: The roof layout workbench needs precise drag-and-drop, rotation, and multi-select gestures that work best on a desktop or tablet. Switch devices to edit your layout.

loading.mappingPanels: Mapping solar panels to your roof...

canvas.title: Roof Layout Workbench
canvas.titleTooltip: Use the slider to add or remove panels. Click a panel on the canvas to select it, then rotate or delete it. Hold spacebar to pan the view
canvas.recalculating: Recalculating energy for new panel dimensions...

sunDirection.tooltip: Approximate sun direction for this month in Malaysia. The amber glow on the canvas shows where sunlight hits your roof.

hint.title: How to use the workbench
hint.subtitle: Try these gestures to shape your layout.
hint.click: Click a panel to select it
hint.drag: Drag any panel to reposition it
hint.zoom: Scroll to zoom in and out
hint.pan: Hold Space and drag to pan the view
hint.dismissCta: Click anywhere or press Esc to dismiss

sidebar.description: Adjust the suggested layout before moving to financial analysis.
sidebar.imageryBaseTooltip: High-resolution satellite imagery was unavailable for this address. Panel positions and flux estimates are approximate.

stats.annualYieldTooltip: Total estimated electricity your panels will generate in a year.
stats.co2OffsetTooltip: Estimated using a factor of {{factor}} kg/MWh based on the grid emission factor for this region.

batchStatus.loading: Computing monthly energy data for all panels...
batchStatus.error: Could not compute monthly energy breakdown. Annual estimates will be used instead.

panelQuantity.label: Panel Quantity
panelQuantity.tooltip: Adjust how many panels to include. Higher-yield panels are kept first when you reduce the count.
panelQuantity.hint: Higher-yield panels are kept visible first. Lowering the slider removes lower-ranked panels from the saved layout.

selectedPanel.label: Selected Panel
selectedPanel.tooltip: Click any panel on the canvas to select it. Hold Shift to select multiple. You can then rotate or delete them.
selectedPanel.multiSelectedHint: Use the rotation slider to rotate all selected panels. Press Delete to remove them.

panelDrawer.drawerDescription: The model selection changes the size and energy output of all panels on your roof.

layoutPresetModal.title: Layout Preset
layoutPresetModal.description: Help us right-size your system. Presets only adjust how many panels are active. They don't move panel positions. Skip to keep the maximum-coverage default.

monthlyBill.label: Monthly Electricity Bill
monthlyBill.tooltip: Find this on your TNB bill. We translate it to kWh using the average TNB tariff to size your system. Look for "Purata Penggunaan" on your bill:
monthlyBill.tnbBillAlt: TNB bill showing average kWh usage

roofDirection.label: Roof Direction
roofDirection.tooltip: Limit panels to a specific roof aspect. "Best Available" places panels on the highest-yield roof faces (typically south for Malaysia).

sizingGoal.label: Sizing Goal
sizingGoal.tooltip: Economy keeps the system small for fastest budget recovery. Self-Consumption sizes the array to match your typical daytime usage. Maximum fills every available panel slot.

conservative.title: Economy
conservative.description: Smallest viable system. Lowest upfront cost, longer payback. Good if budget is the priority.

balanced.title: Self-Consumption
balanced.description: Sized to match your typical daytime usage. Most cost-efficient, minimizes generation that goes to waste from overnight export.

maximum.title: Maximum
maximum.description: Every panel that fits. Largest investment, biggest absolute savings, useful for installers or future-proofing.

tour.step1Body: The blue rectangles are solar panels placed on your roof by satellite analysis. You can tweak this layout before calculating your savings. Hover the info icons beside each control for deeper detail.
tour.step2Body: Tell us your monthly bill and savings goal, and we'll right-size the layout for you. You can re-open this anytime, or skip to keep the maximum-coverage default.
tour.step3Body: Pick a panel model and count from the sidebar, then click any panel on the canvas to drag, rotate or delete it.
tour.step4Body: The toolbar handles the rest: undo/redo, multi-select to edit groups of panels at once, snap alignment for clean edges, zoom, and layer toggles for flux and satellite overlays.
tour.step5Body: Happy with the layout? Click "Save & Continue" to move on to the savings analysis. You can always come back to adjust.
tour.step6Body: Stuck or curious? Tap Sol, your friendly solar guide, to ask anything about this layout, your savings, NEM credits, or how solar works in Malaysia.
